populations.label: Mobility and Strength
populations.functional_profile: Wheelchair users (manual and power), scooter users, ambulant users with walking aids (sticks, crutches, frames, rollators), users with lower limb weakness, balance impairment, or reduced walking endurance.
primary_barriers: Inadequate clear widths and turning spaces

key_parameters.value: ≥1800mm (power); ≥1700mm (best practice); ≥1500mm (code min)
key_parameters.note: ADA 1524mm under-accommodates power wheelchair at 50th percentile

key_parameters.value: ≤1:20 ideal; ≤1:16 intermediate; ≤1:12 code min
900mm best practice; 850mm minimum
0mm target; >13mm gait interception; >20mm 45.8% wheelchair failure
key_parameters.note: Above 20mm door is not effectively accessible

key_parameters.value: Two vertical bars; bilateral; ≥1.3kN load
key_parameters.note: ADA horizontal config is least preferred in clinical literature

key_parameters.value: ≤50m intervals (general); ≤25m (OFS/PAIN primary)

populations.conflict_summary: Zero conflict flags with any other population. MOB provisions are beneficial or neutral for all categories.
populations.co_occurrence_notes: Most common compounds: MOB+PAIN, MOB+OFS, MOB+DEM (ageing). No conflicts.
evidence_sources: Clemson et al. 2023 Cochrane (38% fall reduction)
populations.co1_gap_note: KR and BR Co-1 sources not retrieved

populations.label: Upper Limb and Amputation
populations.functional_profile: Unilateral/bilateral amputation; hemiplegia; upper limb weakness/tremor (MS, stroke, Parkinson's, ABI); reduced grip strength; rheumatoid arthritis; repetitive strain.
primary_barriers: Kitchen/bathroom requiring sustained reach or two-hand operation

key_parameters.value: Lever handles; D-pulls at 850mm AFF; max 22N; 32-45mm diameter
key_parameters.note: No round knobs or simultaneous two-hand operation

key_parameters.value: One-handed operation throughout
key_parameters.note: CRITICAL GAP: zero indexed peer-reviewed studies on one-handed kitchen/bathroom design in any language

key_parameters.value: 760mm from nearest side wall; bilateral fold-down grab bars at 813mm
key_parameters.note: ADA 457mm centreline designed for minority transfer method

populations.conflict_summary: Grab bar diameter range 32-45mm serves both standard MOB grip and UPL compromised grip.
populations.co_occurrence_notes: Frequently co-occurs with MOB/AMB (stroke, MS) and NEU.
populations.evidence_confidence: MODERATE (turning/grab bar); LOW (one-handed kitchen/bathroom — THIN BASE)
populations.co1_gap_note: UPL-specific Co-1 retrieval not yet conducted

populations.label: Vision Impairment
populations.functional_profile: Spectrum from partial sight (low vision — majority) to total blindness. Both groups require specific but different provisions simultaneously.

key_parameters.value: ≥30 LRV points at critical junctions
≥300 lux in circulation; no glare (UGR <19); no transitions >5:1
ISO 23599:2019 truncated dome; ≥4-5mm raised; ≥70% LRV contrast
Braille + tactile text at 1400-1600mm AFF
key_parameters.note: Audio wayfinding at entries and lift calls

populations.conflict_summary: LRV contrast vs NDV muted palette (zoned resolution); TWSI vs PAIN vibration (safety-critical governs); signage density vs NDV visual clutter (pictogram system resolves).
populations.co_occurrence_notes: VIS is independent from DEAF — DBL is distinct category, not VIS+DEAF.
populations.evidence_confidence: HIGH (TWSI, LRV — Tier 4-6 universal); MODERATE (lighting thresholds)
populations.co1_gap_note: RNIB HQ user trials confirmed (UK). Other jurisdictions not systematically retrieved.

populations.label: Deaf and Hard of Hearing
populations.functional_profile: Profoundly deaf; hard of hearing (typically aided); cochlear implant users; hearing loop/assistive listening system users. CI users have distinct acoustic requirements.
primary_barriers: Inadequate room acoustics (high RT60, high background noise)

key_parameters.value: IEC 60118-4:2014+AMD1:2017 in all assembly/reception/counter/communication spaces
key_parameters.note: Highest cross-language convergence of any DEAF specification

key_parameters.value: ≤0.4s at 500Hz-4kHz in speech spaces; ≤0.3s where NDV co-occurs
NC-25 or below
≥0.60 at furthest occupied seat (not room average)
Strobe VAD ≥110 cd throughout occupied spaces
≥1500mm for signing pairs; ≥2400mm on primary signing routes

populations.conflict_summary: Amplification vs NDV noise sensitivity (zoned + IEC); glazed junctions vs DEM reflections (matte glazing); open sightlines vs NDV enclosure (graduated spatial model).
populations.co_occurrence_notes: DEAF provisions generally benefit all populations (visual redundancy). CI-specific requirements may conflict with NDV acoustic targets.
populations.evidence_confidence: HIGH (IEC 60118-4, RT60 — Tier 4-5 across 12+ jurisdictions + Tier 1 CI support); MODERATE (Auracast — transition technology)

populations.label: Neurological and Acquired Brain Injury
populations.functional_profile: ABI; stroke sequelae (unilateral neglect, navigation impairment, aphasia, hemiplegia); MS; Parkinson's; epilepsy; PCS. Most heterogeneous category. 25% of stroke survivors retain navigation impairment at 12 months.

key_parameters.value: ≤2 directional changes per route segment; distinctive landmark at each decision point
≥1200mm min; ≥1500mm preferred (post-stroke)
≤18°C for MS (Uhthoff's — safety-critical)
No flickering; no fluorescent; ≥5m transition zones at illuminance changes

populations.conflict_summary: MS ambient ≤18°C vs PAIN warm preference — safety-critical governs; individual supplemental heating for PAIN.
populations.co_occurrence_notes: Co-occurrence with MOB most frequent in clinical practice.
populations.co1_gap_note: 13 other language communities not yet searched

populations.label: Dementia
populations.functional_profile: Progressive impairment of cognitive mapping, spatial orientation, memory, recognition, executive function. Floor plan configuration is the most influential environmental factor — signage alone cannot compensate for poor layout.

key_parameters.value: Single-corridor or continuous-loop circulation for DEM residential
key_parameters.note: Marquardt and Schmieg 2009; Bowes et al. 2019

key_parameters.value: ≥300 lux at eye level; ≥500 lux task; ≥250 melanopic EDI
≥30 LRV between adjacent surfaces; plain flooring on primary routes
Toilet door visible from bedroom or direct sightline

populations.conflict_summary: Multiple conflicts with NDV (lighting, contrast, signage density, olfactory). Most resolved through three-zone model (F-01). Wandering freedom vs MOB fall supervision resolved residentially but Tier-2-only in institutional settings.
populations.co_occurrence_notes: DEM+MOB most common (ageing). DEM/NDV[IntD-proxy] provisions proxied through DEM+NDV.
populations.evidence_confidence: MODERATE-HIGH (spatial — Tier 3 systematic review); MODERATE (lighting — practitioner guidance); LOW (non-EN evidence — 9-language gaps)
populations.co1_gap_note: EN, DE, NL, JA, FR only. 9 other language communities not searched.

populations.label: Neurodivergence
populations.functional_profile: Autism, ADHD, sensory processing differences, dyslexia, dyspraxia. Common design requirement: user control over sensory environment and protection from uncontrolled sensory input.
primary_barriers: Uncontrolled sensory input (acoustic, visual, olfactory, thermal)

key_parameters.value: A-16: within 25m of any primary occupancy; dimmable 2700-5000K; ≤30 dBA; RT60 ≤0.3s
<55 dB(A) in focused-attention spaces
≤0.3s in NDV-designated spaces
key_parameters.note: Stricter than DEAF ≤0.4s; NDV target governs in co-occurrence

key_parameters.value: NCS chroma ≤4 in shared spaces
Individual control over lighting, acoustic, thermal
key_parameters.note: Highest-impact NDV provision across all subtypes

populations.conflict_summary: Multiple conflicts with DEM and DEAF. Resolved through three-zone sensory model (F-01), hearing loop zoning, graduated spatial model.
populations.co_occurrence_notes: NDV/MH is a separate sub-code with distinct spatial requirements (§2.8). NDV/AUT quiet room ≠ NDV/MH de-escalation room.
populations.evidence_confidence: MODERATE (acoustic — Tier 3-4); LOW (lighting/colour — no validated thresholds); MODERATE-HIGH (spatial/process — ASPECTSS, PAS 6463). All quantified thresholds PROVISIONAL.
populations.co1_gap_note: ZH, NL, FI, IT Co-1 not retrieved

populations.label: Mental Health, PTSD, and Trauma
populations.functional_profile: Anxiety disorders, PTSD, depression, acute psychiatric conditions, trauma histories. Built environment exerts direct, measurable effect on outcomes — ward design variables are primary predictors of seclusion/restraint independently of clinical variables.
primary_barriers: No visual exit (entrapment perception — PTSD critical)

key_parameters.value: Per patient — most robustly evidenced environmental intervention
key_parameters.note: van der Schaaf 2013 — largest effect among 14 design variables, n=23,868

key_parameters.value: Design occupancy = maximum occupancy; no surge without space
No dead ends; all routes loop or terminate at named destinations; exit always visible
≥9m²; visual privacy; exit sightline; RT60 ≤0.4s; warm default CCT
H-02 individual controls in all patient bedrooms and retreat spaces
Warm, nature-derived colours benefit recovery
key_parameters.note: Direct conflict with NDV/AUT muted palette — spatial separation only

populations.conflict_summary: Warm biophilic palette (NDV/MH) vs muted palette (NDV/AUT) — no compromise palette; spatial separation required.
populations.co_occurrence_notes: NDV/MH de-escalation room is distinct from NDV/AUT sensory room — cannot be combined.
populations.evidence_confidence: HIGH (private space, capacity, user control — Tier 1); MODERATE (spatial sequence — Tier 2-4 TID); LOW (colour/nature — Tier 3 + Co-1)
evidence_sources: van der Schaaf et al. 2013 (Tier 1, n=23,868)
populations.co1_gap_note: Wilson 2023 Co-1 retrieved (EN). Other jurisdictions not searched.

populations.label: Chronic Pain and Fibromyalgia
populations.functional_profile: Fibromyalgia, CRPS, chronic widespread pain. Pain amplification, allodynia, fatigue, functional variability. No indexed built environment design research exists in any jurisdiction.

key_parameters.value: Warm ambient preferred (22-24°C)
key_parameters.note: Directly conflicts with NEU/MS ≤18°C — safety-critical governs

key_parameters.value: Insulated coatings on all metal grab bars/handles
Resilient/cushioned (cork, rubber, cushioned vinyl)
≤25m interval (stricter than MOB 50m)

populations.conflict_summary: Thermal conflict with NEU/MS (safety-critical governs; individual warmth for PAIN). TWSI vibration sensitivity (safety-critical VIS/DBL governs on primary routes; JIS T9251 dome sizing partially resolves).
populations.co_occurrence_notes: PAIN+MOB common. PAIN+OFS frequent. PAIN provisions all carry EXPERT CONSENSUS marker.
populations.evidence_confidence: LOW throughout — structural evidence gap. Zero indexed built-environment studies in any language. All provisions extrapolated from clinical evidence.
populations.co1_gap_note: No design-specific Co-1 evidence in any jurisdiction. 7 of 14 languages not attempted.

populations.label: DeafBlind
populations.functional_profile: Combined vision and hearing impairment. NOT VIS+DEAF — distinct category requiring tactile-first design. Visual and auditory provisions are redundancy layers, not primary channels. Communication modes: tactile sign, Protactile, hand-over-hand, residual vision close-range signing, braille.
primary_barriers: Corridors too narrow for intervenor pair
Environmental rearrangement destroying tactile cognitive maps

key_parameters.value: All spatial information accessible through touch alone
≥1500mm on all primary routes (intervenor pair)
Face-to-face ≤600mm; armless movable chairs; ≥1500mm clear per pair
Embossed/thermoform at all major decision points; 900-1200mm AFF
Throughout all occupied spaces as complement to visual strobes
Furniture permanence is a design obligation
key_parameters.note: DBL users build cognitive maps from repeated tactile experience

populations.conflict_summary: DBL reinforces VIS TWSI requirement. No unique conflicts beyond those already in VIS.
populations.co_occurrence_notes: DBL is always a compound condition. Requires specialist consultant (Part 9 §9.11).
populations.evidence_confidence: LOW — Tier 4-5 exclusively. No OT clinical research on DBL-specific built environment in any searched language.
populations.co1_gap_note: Co-1 from US Protactile community only (Clark, Nuccio; EN). All other jurisdictions not searched.

populations.label: Orthostatic and Fatigue Spectrum
populations.functional_profile: ME/CFS, POTS, MCAS. Defining characteristic: post-exertional malaise (PEM) — symptom worsening disproportionate to exertion, persisting 24+ hours. Prolonged standing is primary trigger for POTS orthostatic intolerance; hot environments worsen all OFS subtypes. MCAS co-occurs with chemical/fragrance sensitivity.
primary_barriers: Prolonged standing requirements (queuing, standing-only counters, insufficient seating)

key_parameters.value: ≤25-30m intervals; proportion with 0-30° recline; seated waiting for queues
Cool ≤19-21°C in transit spaces
key_parameters.note: Aligns with NEU/MS Uhthoff threshold — no conflict

key_parameters.value: F-06 mandatory wherever OFS/MCAS is primary/significant secondary
Accessible without purchase in public buildings ≥500m² GFA; ≥8m²
key_parameters.note: Shared with NDV/AUT A-16 — no conflict

key_parameters.value: ≥5m light transition zones (B-05); no direct overhead fluorescent; UGR <19

populations.conflict_summary: OFS provisions have no unique conflicts. OFS thermal requirement aligns with NEU/MS. OFS rest seating is stricter than MOB but directionally compatible.
populations.co_occurrence_notes: OFS+PAIN frequent. OFS+MOB common. OFS rest spaces compatible with NDV quiet rooms.
populations.evidence_confidence: MODERATE (seating, thermal — Co-1/Tier 3); LOW (spatial parameters — zero indexed studies). All provisions carry Tier 5 marker.
populations.co1_gap_note: No design-specific Co-1 evidence in any jurisdiction. All provisions extrapolated from clinical evidence.
